topic.what: First systematic visual classification of gastric mucosal inflammation using the semi-flexible gastroscope.
topic.how: Direct endoscopic observation and grading of mucosal changes (superficial, atrophic, hypertrophic) correlated with clinical findings.
topic.why_matters: Made gastritis a visually diagnosable entity rather than a clinical inference — direct precursor to the Sydney System and all modern endoscopic classification.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of distinct gastritis subtypes defined
characteristics.vs_baseline: First endoscopic classification of any kind

novelty: First visual gastritis classification based on direct observation
Pioneered semi-flexible gastroscope enabling routine gastric visualization
Established endoscopy as diagnostic tool for gastritis
open_problems: Inter-observer variability of visual grading not assessed
